/**
 * Handles the find_symbol MCP tool call.
 * Searches .knowledge/symbols.json for entries matching the provided name
 * (case-insensitive substring match). Optionally filters by symbol type and module.
 * Returns up to 50 results ranked by match quality.
 */

mcp-server/tools/find-template-file.ts.detailedPurpose: Handler for the `find_template_file` MCP tool. Exports: handler.

publicAPI.name: handler
publicAPI.jsdoc:
/**
 * Handler for the `find_template_file` MCP tool.
 *
 * Given a plain-English description, finds the most similar existing files to
 * use as templates for new code, helping maintain consistency with codebase
 * conventions.
 *
 * Strategy selection:
 *   1. Vector similarity (ANN over files.lance) when VectorStore is available
 *   2. Token-matching over summary cache when vectors are unavailable
 *
 * Both paths use the buildResponse()/Section pattern for budget-aware output.
 */

publicAPI.name: handler
handler
handler
handler
handler

publicAPI.name: getOrLoad
publicAPI.jsdoc:
/**
 * Get a cached value or load it from disk.
 *
 * (a) Cache hit + timestamps match → return cached value (increment hits)
 * (b) Cache hit + timestamp differs → full cache invalidation, then reload
 * (c) Cache miss → call loader(), cache result (increment misses)
 *
 * @param key Cache key string
 * @param loader Function that loads data from disk; returns T or null
 * @param currentTimestamp index.lastBuilt timestamp string
 */

publicAPI.name: invalidate
setMaxEntries
publicAPI.jsdoc:

 * Override the max entries limit at runtime.
 * Immediately evicts oldest entries if current size exceeds new limit.

publicAPI.name: loadVectorStore
publicAPI.signature: export async function loadVectorStore(knowledgeRoot: string): Promise<VectorStore | null>
publicAPI.jsdoc:
/**
 * Attempts to open the LanceDB VectorStore at .knowledge/vectors/.
 *
 * Returns null when:
 *   - The vectors directory does not exist (embeddings not yet built)
 *   - @lancedb/lancedb is not installed (optional dependency)
 *
 * Errors from LanceDB are caught and re-thrown with a user-friendly message.
 *
 * NOTE: This function is async because LanceDB requires async connection setup.
 */

publicAPI.name: buildBM25IndexFromKnowledge
publicAPI.jsdoc:
/**
 * Builds an in-memory BM25 index from the knowledge base.
 *
 * Documents are indexed as:
 *   - 'file:<relPath>'     → llmDescription / detailedPurpose / purpose
 *   - 'symbol:<qualName>' → signature + jsdoc
 *   - 'feature:<id>'      → name + description
 *
 * Returns an empty index (documentCount === 0) when no knowledge data is available.
 */

publicAPI.name: loadKnowledgeGraph
publicAPI.signature: export function loadKnowledgeGraph(knowledgeRoot: string): KnowledgeGraphData | null
publicAPI.jsdoc:
/**
 * Loads the knowledge graph, using a two-level strategy:
 *
 *  1. Try pre-built files: .knowledge/graph/nodes.json + .knowledge/graph/edges.json
 *     (written by Phase 8 of the build pipeline).
 *  2. Fallback: build in-memory from symbols, dependencies, summaries, and features.
 *
 * Returns null only if neither strategy succeeds (no knowledge data at all).
 * Results are cached per index build timestamp.
 */

publicAPI.name: buildFileTree

/**
 * Resolves a user-provided path against projectRoot and verifies containment.

 * Returns the resolved absolute path if safe, null if it escapes the project boundary.

 * @param projectRoot Absolute project root path
 */

publicAPI.name: computeClosestMatches
publicAPI.jsdoc:
/**
 * Returns the top-N candidates from `candidates` with the highest substring
 * overlap to `query`. Uses trigram scoring (shared 3-char substrings),
 * normalized by max(query.length, candidate.length).
 *
 * No external edit-distance library — pure string operations.
 *
 * @param query      The search query
 * @param candidates Pool of candidate strings to rank
 * @param topN       Number of top results to return (default: 3)
 */

mcp-server/tools/lib/response-budget.ts.detailedPurpose: Builds a response string from sections, respecting the byte budget. Exports: buildResponse, Section.

publicAPI.name: buildResponse
publicAPI.signature: export function buildResponse(sections: Section[], toolBudget?: number): string
publicAPI.jsdoc:
/**
 * Builds a response string from sections, respecting the byte budget.
 * Sections are sorted by priority (lower = more important).
 * If budget is exceeded, whole sections are dropped starting from lowest priority.
 * Never cuts mid-sentence — drops entire sections with a truncation marker.
 *
 * @param sections Array of sections to include
 * @param toolBudget Optional per-tool budget override (defaults to DEFAULT_BUDGET)
 * @returns Formatted response string within budget
 */

publicAPI.name: buildCallGraph
publicAPI.signature: export function buildCallGraph(project: Project, symbols: SymbolEntry[], projectRoot?: string): SymbolEntry[]
publicAPI.jsdoc:
/**
 * Builds a call graph by identifying project symbols called by each symbol.
 * Does not mutate the input array.
 */

publicAPI.name: rebuildCallGraphForFile
publicAPI.jsdoc:
/**
 * Rebuilds call graph entries for a single changed file, then inverts the full graph.
 * More efficient than rebuilding the entire call graph for incremental updates.
 */

publicAPI.name: rebuildCallGraphForFiles
publicAPI.jsdoc:
/**
 * Rebuilds call graph entries for multiple changed files without inverting.
 * Used by batch processing to avoid cross-file staleness: all files are processed
 * against the same symbol set, then a single invertCallGraph pass is done by the caller.
 * Does not mutate the input array.
 */

publicAPI.name: invertCallGraph
publicAPI.jsdoc:
/**
 * Inverts the call graph to populate the 'calledBy' array for each symbol.
 * Does not mutate the input array.
 */

scripts/lib/incremental-updater.ts.detailedPurpose: Re-processes a single changed file: removes old symbol entries, re-extracts symbols, Exports: handleFileChange, handleBatchFileChanges, handleFileDeletion.

publicAPI.name: handleFileChange
publicAPI.jsdoc:
/**
 * Re-processes a single changed file: removes old symbol entries, re-extracts symbols,
 * rebuilds call graph entries, updates summaries cache, and writes both artifacts atomically.
 * Must complete in under 1 second for a single file.
 */

publicAPI.name: handleBatchFileChanges
publicAPI.jsdoc:
/**
 * Processes multiple interdependent file changes in a single pass to avoid cross-file
 * call graph staleness. When files A and B both change in one batch, this ensures that
 * A's call graph is rebuilt against B's fresh symbols (and vice versa), then a single
 * invertCallGraph pass produces consistent calledBy edges.
 */

publicAPI.name: handleFileDeletion

scripts/lib/python/python-call-graph.ts.detailedPurpose: Builds a regex-based call graph for Python symbols. Exports: buildPythonCallGraph.

publicAPI.name: buildPythonCallGraph
publicAPI.jsdoc:
/**
 * Builds a regex-based call graph for Python symbols.
 *
 * This is intentionally imprecise — it captures most direct calls but won't
 * catch aliased or dynamic calls. The caller (build-knowledge.ts) is expected
 * to run `invertCallGraph()` on the merged result, so this function only
 * populates `calls` arrays.
 *
 * @param symbols     All known Python SymbolEntry objects (will NOT be mutated).
 * @param fileContents Map of absolute file path -> file source text.
 * @param projectRoot Absolute path to the project root (used for relativising paths).
 * @returns A new array of SymbolEntry objects with `calls` populated.
 */

publicAPI.name: extractPythonDeps
publicAPI.signature: export function extractPythonDeps(filePath: string, content: string, projectRoot: string): ImportInfo[]

mcp-server/tools/lib/working-memory.ts.detailedPurpose: Retrieve a cached value. Exports: getFromMemory, setInMemory, clearMemory, getMemoryStats.

publicAPI.name: getFromMemory
publicAPI.signature: export function getFromMemory<T>(key: string, currentBuildTimestamp: string): T | undefined
publicAPI.jsdoc:
/**
 * Retrieve a cached value.
 *
 * Returns undefined when:
 *   - Key not present (cache miss)
 *   - Entry TTL has expired (lazy cleanup)
 *   - buildTimestamp differs from the current index (rebuild detected)
 *
 * On a hit: refreshes LRU position, returns structuredClone of value.
 */

publicAPI.name: setInMemory
publicAPI.signature: export function setInMemory<T>(key: string, value: T, buildTimestamp: string): void
publicAPI.jsdoc:
/**
 * Store a value in working memory.
 *
 * If the store is at capacity (50 entries), the oldest entry is evicted first.
 * @param key               Cache key (should be deterministic for the same query+scope+topK)
 * @param value             Value to cache
 * @param buildTimestamp    Current index.lastBuilt string (used for invalidation)
 */

publicAPI.name: clearMemory
publicAPI.jsdoc: /** Remove all entries from working memory. */

publicAPI.name: getMemoryStats
publicAPI.jsdoc: /** Returns observability stats. */

publicAPI.jsdoc:
/**
 * Minimal EmbeddingProvider for the MCP server — makes HTTP calls to
 * Ollama (default) or OpenAI to embed query strings at search time.
 *
 * This is separate from scripts/lib/embeddings/ because tsconfig.mcp.json
 * uses rootDir="mcp-server" and cannot import from scripts/.
 *
 * Only the embed() method is needed for query-time search; upsert / batch
 * functionality lives in the build-pipeline providers (scripts/).
 */
/** Minimal interface needed by the MCP-server hybrid search path. */

publicAPI.jsdoc:
/**
 * Pure RRF merge of multiple ranked lists.
 *
 * @param rankings  Each element is a ranked list of {id, rank} pairs (rank is 1-based).
 * @param k         RRF constant (default 60, as recommended by the original paper).
 * @returns         Merged list sorted by descending RRF score.
 */

publicAPI.name: hybridSearch
publicAPI.signature:
export async function hybridSearch(
    query: string,
    scope: SearchScope,
    topK: number,
    vectorStore: VectorStore,
    bm25: BM25Index,
    embeddingProvider: EmbeddingProvider
): Promise<HybridSearchResult[]>
publicAPI.jsdoc:

 * @param vectorStore       LanceDB-backed vector store (must be isAvailable())
 * @param bm25              In-memory BM25 index pre-populated from knowledge data
 * @param embeddingProvider Provider used to embed the query string

 */

mcp-server/tools/lib/knowledge-graph.ts.detailedPurpose: Loads the pre-built knowledge graph from: Exports: loadGraph, buildKnowledgeGraph, traverseGraph, resolveStartNodes.

publicAPI.name: loadGraph
publicAPI.signature: export function loadGraph(knowledgeRoot: string): KnowledgeGraphData | null
publicAPI.jsdoc:
/**
 * Loads the pre-built knowledge graph from:
 *   .knowledge/graph/nodes.json
 *   .knowledge/graph/edges.json
 *
 * Returns null if either file is missing or unreadable.
 */

publicAPI.name: buildKnowledgeGraph
publicAPI.signature:
export function buildKnowledgeGraph(
    symbols: SymbolEntry[],
    dependencies: DependencyGraph,
    summaries: Record<string, FileSummary>,
    projectRoot: string,
    features?: FeatureGroup[]
): KnowledgeGraphData
publicAPI.jsdoc:
/**
 * Builds a KnowledgeGraphData in-memory from existing knowledge artifacts.
 *
 * This is the fallback path used when pre-built graph/nodes.json and
 * graph/edges.json have not been generated by Phase 8 of the build pipeline.
 *
 * Edge construction:
 *   contains    — file → symbol (symbol belongs to file)
 *   calls       — symbol → symbol (call graph from SymbolEntry.calls)
 *   imports     — file → file (from DependencyGraph.fileDeps)
 *   depends_on  — module → module (from DependencyGraph.edges)
 *   similar_to  — feature → feature (from FeatureGroup.relatedFeatures)
 */

publicAPI.name: traverseGraph
publicAPI.signature:
export function traverseGraph(
    graph: KnowledgeGraphData,
    startId: string,
    edgeTypes: string[],
    maxDepth: number,
    direction: 'outgoing' | 'incoming' | 'both'
): TraversalResult
publicAPI.jsdoc:
/**
 * BFS traversal over the knowledge graph starting from startId.
 *
 * @param graph        The KnowledgeGraphData to traverse
 * @param startId      Node ID to start from (must be exact match)
 * @param edgeTypes    Edge type filter — only these edges are followed
 * @param maxDepth     Maximum BFS depth (1–5)
 * @param direction    'outgoing' follows source→target, 'incoming' follows target→source,
 *                     'both' follows both directions
 *
 * Returns an empty result when startId is not found in the graph.
 * Caps at MAX_TRAVERSAL_NODES to keep responses within budget.
 */

publicAPI.name: resolveStartNodes
publicAPI.signature: export function resolveStartNodes(graph: KnowledgeGraphData, start: string): string[]
publicAPI.jsdoc:
/**
 * Resolves a user-supplied "start" string to matching node IDs in the graph.
 *
 * Resolution order:
 *  1. Exact ID match (e.g. "file:tools/cache.ts", "symbol:handler")
 *  2. Prefix-qualified match (e.g. "tools/cache.ts" → tries "file:tools/cache.ts")
 *  3. Suffix match for files (e.g. "cache.ts" matches any file ending in that)
 *  4. Symbol name match (case-insensitive name or qualifiedName)
 *  5. Module match
 *
 * Returns an empty array when nothing matches.
 */

publicAPI.name: createEmbeddingProvider
publicAPI.jsdoc:
/**
 * Creates an EmbeddingProvider based on environment configuration.
 *
 * Environment variables:
 *   EMBEDDING_MODEL   — 'ollama' (default) or 'openai'
 *   OLLAMA_MODEL      — Ollama model name (default: 'nomic-embed-text')
 *   OLLAMA_BASE_URL   — Ollama base URL (default: 'http://localhost:11434')
 *   EMBEDDING_CONCURRENCY — concurrent HTTP requests for Ollama (default: 10)
 *   OPENAI_API_KEY    — required when EMBEDDING_MODEL=openai
 *
 * @throws if EMBEDDING_MODEL=openai and OPENAI_API_KEY is not set
 */
